title: crepis juvenalis
intro: Crepis juvenalis, also known as young hawk's beard or baby's breath, is a flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae) characterized by its small white flowers and fern-like foliage. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Crepis juvenalis include reducing inflammation, improving respiratory issues, and supporting the immune system. Herbal preparations of Crepis juvenalis can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to alleviate symptoms of colds, flu, and other respiratory infections.
